question: E-004-resilience
claim: DL-idempotent-self-resume PARTIAL/NO-GO: runtime is a fire-and-exit batch orchestrator (fanout-run.cjs:378,506 process.exit per run; loop-lock only gives restart-survival via TTL staleness), NOT a self-prompting daemon. No self-prompt/arm token anywhere; the candidate presumes a persistent self-prompt queue + delivery-confirmation that doesn't exist — greenfield, wrong substrate.

question: E-004-resilience
claim: DL-graceful-self-stop GO: 'empty-tick=valid' REAL (fanout-run.cjs:370-373 + pool:155-159). But 'mid-tick preserves in-flight' REFUTED — SIGINT/SIGTERM exit 130/143 with no-op cleanup (:354) kills children with no drain/salvage. Fix = flush a partial summary with a 'stopped' marker so exit-code/summary consumers don't read it as clean success.

question: E-004-resilience
claim: DL-failure-class-taxonomy GO: timeout-vs-exit IS computed upstream (fanout-run.cjs:472-491, failure carries .timedOut/.exitCode/.salvage) but FLATTENED in settleItem (fanout-pool.cjs:100-117 keeps only {name,message}); class survives only as message substring, salvage-miss not a label. Fix = stop discarding props + class rollup in buildPoolSummary (low effort; summary additions safe, error-shape change touches the shared settle contract).

question: E-004-resilience
claim: DL-progress-heartbeat PARTIAL/NEEDS-BENCHMARK: per-item started/completed/failed events exist (orchestration-status.log) but no periodic heartbeat WITHIN a long single lineage (one started, one completed for a multi-minute child); loop-lock heartbeat is LOCK-TTL not progress. Benchmark whether long-lineage stalls are real pain; the shutdown-summary half is GO (pairs with #2).

question: E-004-resilience
claim: Deep-loop is a fire-and-exit BATCH orchestrator (not a daemon) -> self-resume wrong-substrate NO-GO. Two clean GOs: graceful-self-stop (flush 'stopped'-marked partial summary; children die silently today) + failure-class-taxonomy (timeout/exit class computed then discarded before summary). newInfoRatio 0.50.
